dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Establish the cramped domestic world, the found-family bond, and the economic precarity shaping every choice.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: The apartment's intimacy and financial strain are established, and Moth clocks that Rowan is acting normal a little too hard.

events.text: The apartment is one room pretending to be two: kettle hissing, rent notice under the sugar tin, Moth's coveralls draped over a chair.
Keys, badge, mop charms. Rent never takes a night off, so neither do I.
Then tell the rent notice to stop staring at us. It's ruining the decor.
You were supposed to be asleep.
I was. Then the kettle started making martyr noises, and I caved.
You're trying awful hard to sound fine.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Trigger the scene's central concern by making Rowan's illness impossible to ignore.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Denial becomes harder to maintain; the conversation has to turn toward Rowan's condition.

events.text: Rowan pushes up from the couch toward the mugs. Halfway standing, their balance goes strange.
A cough folds them in two. When they pull their sleeve away, silvery residue glints against the wool.
Rowan.
Don't say it like that. I'm still upright.
At Rowan's temples, the glamour smoothing their skin flickers thin, silver cracks showing before they force it steady.
That is not ordinary tired.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Create the first emotional confrontation and let the player define Moth's approach to care.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Rowan stops pretending it is only a bad night and admits there is a wider pattern.

events.text: Don't joke me past this.
I am deeply offended you think I would use charm in a medical emergency.
Maybe it's a cold. A weird one. A glamorous cold.
Be honest with me.

events.prompt: How does Moth push Rowan to be honest?

branch_events.text: Please. I need the truth, not the version that makes it easier for me to leave.
It's been days.
And I'm not the only one. A few fae I know from night work are getting drained the same way.

options.option_text: Use dry humor to lower Rowan's guard.

branch_events.text: Rowan, I live with you. If there were a glamorous cold, you'd already be charging it rent.
Cruel. Accurate. Fine.
It's been days, and other fae workers are going thin too. Loading docks, kitchens, cleaners.

options.option_text: Push bluntly and demand the truth.

branch_events.text: Stop lying to me. You coughed silver into your sleeve.
All right.
It's been days. And it's not just me. Other fae on shift work are getting hollowed out too.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Define the illness in emotionally specific terms and link it to the story's themes of self-erasure.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth understands the illness as something terrifyingly intimate: it attacks the self, not just the body.

events.text: Rowan sinks back down and presses a thumb to one temple like they can hold the cracks shut by hand.
It doesn't feel like being sick the human way.
It feels like something's rubbing me smooth. Little bits go missing first.
A word. A glamour. The part of me that knows when rain is coming.
I kept thinking if I slept hard enough, I'd wake up ordinary enough to keep my job.
That's not ordinary. That's theft.
Yeah. And the worst part is it makes disappearing sound sensible.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Force the practical conflict between care and survival: help, work, rent, and visibility.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth and Rowan reach a fragile plan, but neither feels safe or satisfied with it.

events.text: We're not waiting until this takes more.
And do what, exactly? Go to a hospital and let them call me dehydrated until morning?
You miss one shift, we feel it. I miss more work, we drown.
Then give me a plan I can walk out the door with.

events.prompt: What promise does Moth make before the shift?

options.option_text: Keep the shift, but investigate tonight.

branch_events.text: I keep the shift. Quietly. And I start looking tonight for whatever's doing this.
Fine. Look quiet, then. If this is at work, you'll smell it before anyone else does.

options.option_text: Promise to find a clinic right after work.

branch_events.text: I finish, and at first light we find a clinic. Hidden, illegal, blessed by lint, I don't care.
After work, then. No skipping pay. I can make it to dawn.

options.option_text: Almost call out, then promise to come back fast.

branch_events.text: I should call out and stay. Let Brighthall survive one night without me.
Tempting image. Still no. Go in, come back fast, and let me keep one scrap of dignity.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Close the scene with intimacy, dread, and a clear forward drive into the investigation.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth heads to Brighthall newly alarmed, emotionally raw, and primed to recognize signs that this sickness is larger than Rowan alone.

events.text: Moth sets fresh tea within reach, tucks the blanket higher, and loops a copper charm over the lamp switch.
If it gets worse, call me. Bang on the radiator. Throw something at the wall. I mean it.
Yes, captain.
Come back safe. I'd hate to haunt the apartment alone.
Don't practice haunting until I'm home.
The keys at Moth's belt sound louder than usual when the apartment door finally gives.
Out in the hall, the shift stops being routine. Brighthall is the first place to look.

dialogue_scenes.scene_exit_state: Moth leaves for Brighthall frightened and determined, newly primed to notice that Rowan's sickness is part of something larger affecting fae workers.
dialogue_scenes.notes_and_work_product_summary: Adapted all six source beats into an intimate apartment scene with two local interactive choice points: how Moth gets Rowan to admit the truth, and what promise Moth makes before leaving. Portrait staging keeps only one speaker visible at a time, with narration used for symptom reveals and the final transition into the investigation.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Establish Moth's precarious routine, Rowan's lingering presence in their mind, and the tower's sterile midnight atmosphere.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth is emotionally primed to notice that Brighthall's ordinary nighttime routine feels subtly hostile.

events.text: Midnight turned Brighthall's service floors into a maze of waxed tile, locked glass, and fluorescent hum.
One cart, six floors, no disasters. That's the prayer.
Keep it together, Moth. Rowan needs rent money, not a public breakdown by the recycle bins.
The air coming off the vents was too dry, too sharp, like the building had learned how to clean past clean.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Deliver the first physical clue tying the building to the sickness.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth now has direct evidence that something in the building's airflow is wrong and potentially dangerous to fae.

events.text: At an HVAC return near the conference suites, pale dust furred the grate in a thin white seam.
That is not ordinary dust.
Salt sparkle. Iron grit. Who salts an air vent?
When Moth leaned closer, the little magic stitched into their cuffs shrank back from it.

events.prompt: How does Moth handle the residue in the vent?

options.option_text: Carefully scrape and bag a clean sample.

branch_events.text: Easy. Into the spare bag. No drama.
Moth scraped a careful ribbon of residue loose and sealed it away, losing precious minutes but keeping the sample clean.

options.option_text: Test it with a small charm first.

branch_events.text: Tiny charm, do me one favor.
The charm hissed against the dust and dimmed at once. Static bit through Moth's glove, leaving a faint chalky trace on the knuckles.

options.option_text: Take only a quick pinch and move on.

branch_events.text: Quick and gone. I can be suspicious later.
Moth pinched only a trace of the residue and wiped the grate clean, leaving as little sign as possible.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Show that the threat is not only physical but bureaucratic, reinforcing the story's erasure theme.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth links the vent residue to a pattern of institutional erasure rather than an isolated maintenance problem.

events.text: The maintenance alcove held a charging tablet, a paper binder, and the stale smell of toner.
Filter replacement, duct seal, after-hours access...
Badge number's there. Work order's there. So why won't the name stay put?
That's not bad filing. That's something practicing erasure.
Every time Moth tried to fix the letters in memory, they thinned away. The building kept the task and misplaced the person.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Introduce Vera as a possible threat or ally and force Moth to choose how much to reveal.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: The confrontation shifts from possible discipline to the possibility of a quiet alliance.

events.text: A flashlight beam skimmed the alcove wall and settled on Moth's stalled cart.
Night janitors usually move faster when they're not doing detective work.
I was between mops.
Uh-huh. You missed a vent by conference C, and you look rattled enough to set off my paperwork reflex.

events.prompt: How much does Moth trust Vera?

options.option_text: Play dumb and keep it vague.

branch_events.text: Long shift. Dust in my eye. That's all.
Then officially, finish your route. Unofficially, quit touching the vents barehanded.
Someone at home is sick. Same dry look, same missing pieces. I need to know if this place is doing it.
Then you're not imagining things. And you're not the first one to ask me that this month.

options.option_text: Press her about the missing names and dust.

branch_events.text: The vent's salted, the records are eating names, and I'm done pretending that's normal. Are you going to write me up or help?
If I meant to write you up, we'd be downstairs already. Lower your voice and keep breathing.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: Expand the stakes beyond Rowan and confirm that Brighthall has a suppressed pattern of harm.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth realizes Rowan's illness is part of a wider pattern tied to labor, invisibility, and Brighthall's management culture.

events.text: Vera guided Moth into the narrow slice of hallway between a dead copier bank and a camera blind spot.
You're not the first overnight worker to notice something wrong.
Cleaners, temp maintenance, loading dock runners. They call out sick, get dropped from the schedule, and the records start looking like they were never here.
Rowan's been fading like that. Not all at once. Just... less there every week.
Brighthall loves invisible labor. Makes it easy to lose invisible people.

dialogue_beats.source_purpose: End the scene with a concrete lead and a fragile new thread of support.
dialogue_beats.source_exit_state: Moth has a harmful sample, proof of altered records, confirmation that others are sick, and directions to the Spin Cycle Clinic.

events.text: There's a place called Spin Cycle. Laundromat on paper, clinic downstairs if you know which bank of dead dryers to pass.
Take that dust sample there. Keep it sealed. Wear gloves, and don't breathe too deep around any vent in this building.
Officially, I never said this. Brighthall prefers places like that not to exist.
Spin Cycle. Got it.
Thank you. I won't hang you out for helping me.
Good. And Moth? Hold on to your own name while you're at it.
Moth rolled the cart back into the fluorescent hush with a sealed sample, a pattern of missing names, and one quiet lead to follow before Rowan slipped any further away.

dialogue_scenes.scene_exit_state: Moth leaves the service floors with direct evidence from Brighthall's vents, proof that names are being thinned out of the maintenance records, confirmation that other invisible workers are sick, and Vera's quiet directions to the Spin Cycle Clinic.
dialogue_scenes.notes_and_work_product_summary: Six source beats were adapted into a concise investigation scene with atmospheric narration, one-speaker portrait staging, and two localized choice moments in beat 2 and beat 4. The dialogue preserves the scene turn from private suspicion to shared pattern, ending on Vera's covert referral to the clinic.

gui_colors.reasoning: I chose a noir-leaning palette grounded in dust, warm tungsten, and rain-washed concrete to match the story’s urban hidden-magic mood. The text colors stay light and slightly warm for readability against dark interfaces, while the accent and selected states use muted brass/copper tones that evoke keys, old wards, and persistence without feeling flashy. Disabled and muted colors are kept low-contrast but still legible, reflecting the themes of invisibility, bureaucracy, and worn-down labor.
